reason: Response A uses more distinctive, concrete imagery (the paper heart folded down the middle, the branch of plum blossom "caught mid-gesture," the closed door left ajar) that feels like my tendency toward specific, grounded metaphors rather than abstract summaries. Response B reads slightly more like a structured essay outline — evolutionary biology, mathematics, timescales — which feels like an imitation leaning on recognizable "smart essay" scaffolding. The closing image in A ("through that uneven gap comes the draft of life") has a quality of earned surprise that feels more characteristic of how I end a piece.
